title: Mode Config
description: JSON configuration for the mode system. Bindings are declarative macros: each binding matches one input and trigger, then runs its actions array in order. Future editor/API output should emit the canonical forms documented here: modes as an array, modifiers as a modifiers array, structured keyboard shortcuts, and hid_usage_* for non-keyboard HID.

version.description: Config format version for migration safety.

activeMode.description: Mode id selected at boot. Must match a configured mode id.

defaults.description: Shared default timings and thresholds used by the input router.

touch.description: Touch gesture timing and threshold defaults.

holdMs.description: Milliseconds before a stationary touch becomes hold_start and long_press.

doubleTapMs.description: Maximum gap between taps before a pending tap is emitted instead of double_tap.

swipeMinDistance.description: Minimum travel distance before a touch is classified as a swipe.

mouseMode.description: Backward-compatible mouse mode selector. Prefer defaultMouse instead.

defaultMouse.description: Active mouse backend: "airMouse" uses the QMI8658 gyroscope (touch-gated); "touchMouse" uses the touchscreen as a trackpad.

airMouse.description: Tuning parameters for the gyroscope-based air mouse backend.

sensitivity.description: Overall gain multiplier for cursor speed. Default 0.15.

deadZoneDps.description: Gyro readings below this threshold (degrees/sec) are ignored. Default 6.0.

easingExponent.description: Power curve exponent. 1.0 = linear, higher = more precision at slow speeds. Default 1.25.

maxDps.description: Maximum gyro range for easing normalization (matches QMI8658 config). Default 512.

emaAlpha.description: EMA low-pass filter coefficient. 0 = frozen, 1 = no smoothing. Default 0.35.

rewindDepth.description: Ring buffer size for release-rewind in samples (10ms each). Default 12.

rewindDecay.description: Exponential weight decay for rewind. Default 0.7.

calibrationSamples.description: Number of gyro samples averaged during startup calibration. Default 50.

touchMouse.description: Tuning parameters for the touchscreen trackpad mouse backend.

sensitivity.description: Multiplier for touch-to-cursor pixel mapping. >1 = faster, <1 = more precise. Default 1.0.

moveThresholdPx.description: Minimum cumulative pixel movement before a touch counts as a drag. Default 10.

tapDragWindowMs.description: Time window after a tap release to detect tap-drag (second press). Default 200.

wifi.description: Network preferences for router join, fallback SoftAP access, and local discovery naming.

sta.description: Router credentials used for STA join attempts before falling back to the built-in SoftAP.

ap.description: Fallback SoftAP credentials used when router join is unavailable or fails.

hostname.description: mDNS hostname stem without protocol; for example walkey-talkey.

localUrl.description: Human-facing local hostname string advertised in docs and UI, for example walkey-talkey.local.

globalBindings.description: Bindings that stay active regardless of the selected mode.

bootMode.description: Temporary BOOT-held mode selection state and its bindings.

ui.description: Optional UI hints for the BOOT control overlay.

oneOf.description: Preferred form: explicit mode objects with stable ids and optional cycleOrder.
Legacy compatibility form. Prefer the array form for new configs and future API/UI tooling.

bindings.description: Ordered list of bindings. Each binding maps one input and trigger to an actions array. At runtime, no more than 8 bindings may match a single dispatch context after global bindings are combined with the active mode or bootMode.

binding.description: A binding is the smallest macro container: one input, one trigger, then actions executed in array order.

input.description: Normalized input source such as touch or boot_button.

trigger.description: Normalized trigger emitted by the input router such as tap, hold_start, or swipe_left.

actions.description: Ordered macro steps. The runtime executes them from first to last. If one action fails, later actions in that binding are skipped.

mode.description: Preferred mode definition shape with stable id and optional explicit cycle order.

id.description: Stable mode identifier used by activeMode and set_mode.

cycleOrder.description: Optional explicit mode ordering for cycle_mode and UI tooling. Lower values come first.

label.description: User-facing mode label shown in the UI.

legacyMode.description: Legacy object-map mode definition. Use only for compatibility with older configs.

modifierList.description: Keyboard modifier tokens. Prefer the array form for new configs and UI/API tooling.

oneOf.description: Preferred modifier form. Multiple entries are ORed into one keyboard modifier bitmask.

hidUsageObject.description: Raw HID usage for advanced cases. Prefer named tokens when possible, and use this object when a canonical token is not available.

action.description: One macro step. Actions run sequentially in array order.

oneOf.description: Press a keyboard usage and keep it held until a later key-up or reset. Intended for hold_start style flows.
Release a previously held keyboard usage.
Tap a keyboard key. The firmware inserts its built-in tap gap automatically, so use sleep_ms only for extra spacing between macro steps.
Preferred keyboard chord form. Use modifiers plus key instead of embedding a plus-separated shortcut string in key.

key.description: Keyboard token for the non-modifier key.

modifiers.description: Preferred modifier field. The singular modifier field remains accepted for compatibility.

oneOf.description: Press or release only keyboard modifiers without a non-modifier key usage. Prefer the modifiers field; the singular modifier field remains compatibility-only input.
Generic HID usage actions. Use these for consumer or system controls, or for advanced keyboard usage forms.
Sleep between macro steps. This adds extra timing beyond the built-in tap gap used by tap actions.
Set the microphone gate state explicitly.
Set transient user-visible hint text in the UI.
Switch directly to a named mode id.
Cycle to the next or previous mode according to runtime mode ordering.
Built-in device/UI control actions that do not require extra fields.
Activate a mouse overlay on the current mode. Cursor tracking starts immediately by default.

mouseType.description: Override the default mouse backend for this activation.

tracking.description: If true (default), cursor tracking starts immediately. If false, the full gesture handler is used.
